description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a broad, double-edged blade with a subtle taper toward a pointed tip. The blade has a dark, metallic sheen with slight reflective qualities, suggesting it is forged from steel or a similar metal. The crossguard is straight and robust, providing hand protection, and is finished in a lighter metallic tone. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a textured material for grip, and ends in a faceted pommel that balances the sword. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements on the blade, guard, or handle. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, suitable for practical use rather than ceremonial display.
description: A broad, double-edged medieval sword with a dark metallic blade, straight crossguard, and a textured cylindrical handle ending in a faceted pommel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the dark, double-edged blade, straight crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a faceted pommel.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the rectangular cross-section of the guard and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the blade's taper, crossguard, and handle details.
description_view_3: Reverse edge-on view focusing on the alignment of the blade, guard, and handle from the pommel end.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, wrapped, faceted pommel, textured grip
